description:
IF  BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 1 AND any cell voltage >= r_CellOV_L3_Threshold
THEN
  · BMS_CellOV_L3_Flag := 1
supplementary_info:
Function: Overvoltage Protection
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery
Context: If the overvoltage condition escalates beyond Level 2 despite derating, immediate contactor opening is required to protect the battery. Contactors are opened in sequence and the system transitions to a safe state:
- BMS_ChargeContactor_State: 0 = Open, 1 = Closed
- BMS_DischargeContactor_State: 0 = Open, 1 = Closed
- BMS_SystemState transitions to SafeState; refer to section 13 for state definitions
- Recovery requires all cell voltages below 4.00 V for >= 5 s

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_CellOV_L3_Flag := 1 when any cell voltage >= r_CellOV_L3_Threshold and BMS_ChargeTerm_Req = 1
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where BMS_ChargeTerm_Req is active, any cell voltage exceeds r_CellOV_L3_Threshold, and BMS_CellOV_L3_Flag transitions to 1.
Test the boundary condition where a cell voltage equals exactly r_CellOV_L3_Threshold when BMS_ChargeTerm_Req is active.
Test the fault condition where multiple cells exceed r_CellOV_L3_Threshold simultaneously while BMS_ChargeTerm_Req is active.
Verify that BMS_SystemState transitions to SafeState when all cell voltages are below 4.00 V for >= 5 s after BMS_CellOV_L3_Flag is set.
Validate the observability of BMS_ChargeContactor_State, BMS_DischargeContactor_State, and BMS_SystemState transitions in response to overvoltage conditions.

description:
IF  BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1 AND charge current already derated to 50 % for >= 1 s
    AND any cell voltage >= r_CellOV_Threshold
THEN
  · BMS_ChargeTerm_Req := 1
supplementary_info:
Function: Overvoltage Protection
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_ChargeTerm_Req := 1 when the conditions are met.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test that BMS_ChargeTerm_Req is set to 1 when BMS_CellOV_Flag is 1, charge current has been derated to 50% for at least 1 second, and any cell voltage reaches r_CellOV_Threshold.
Test that BMS_ChargeTerm_Req remains 0 if BMS_CellOV_Flag is 1 but the charge current has not been derated to 50% for at least 1 second, even if any cell voltage reaches r_CellOV_Threshold.
Test that BMS_ChargeTerm_Req remains 0 if BMS_CellOV_Flag is 1 and the charge current has been derated to 50% for at least 1 second, but no cell voltage reaches r_CellOV_Threshold.
Test that BMS_ChargeTerm_Req remains 0 if BMS_CellOV_Flag is not 1, even if the charge current has been derated to 50% for at least 1 second and any cell voltage reaches r_CellOV_Threshold.
Test that BMS_ChargeTerm_Req remains 0 if an unexpected fault occurs (e.g., a sudden increase in charge current) while the conditions are met.

description:
Upon VCU_HVDisconnect_Req or critical fault:
  · Open both main contactors
  · Discharge DC link capacitance
  · BMS_SystemState := HV_Deactivating
  · THEN Standby or SafeState as appropriate
supplementary_info:
Function: BMS State Machine
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation > 9.2 Charge Termination > 10 Pack Voltage Protection > 10.1 Pack Voltage Monitoring > 11 Degradation Monitoring > 11.1 Cell Degradation Detection > 12 Fault Management > 12.1 Unrecoverable Fault Handling > 13 BMS State Machine > 13.1 Power-Up and Initialization > 13.2 HV Activation > 13.3 HV Deactivation and Sleep
Context: HV deactivation is triggered by normal shutdown requests or fault conditions from any protection function (sections 1-12). After contactors open and the DC link discharges, the system may enter low-power sleep mode to conserve quiescent current while maintaining wake-up capability:
- Deactivation: open contactors, discharge DC link, transition through HV_Deactivating to Standby or SafeState
- Sleep entry: Standby with no CAN activity for configurable timeout
- Sleep exit: CAN activity or wake pin assertion

analysis.direction: The requirement describes a triggering behavior. The BMS transitions to the appropriate state (Standby or SafeState) after certain actions are performed.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test normal HV deactivation sequence: VCU_HVDisconnect_Req is asserted, main contactors open, DC link discharges, BMS_SystemState transitions to HV_Deactivating and then to Standby or SafeState as appropriate.

Test fault condition triggering HV deactivation: a critical fault occurs while VCU_HVDisconnect_Req is not asserted, main contactors open, DC link discharges, BMS_SystemState transitions to HV_Deactivating and then to Standby or SafeState as appropriate.

Test transition from HV_Deactivating state: after the deactivation sequence completes, verify that the system correctly enters either Standby or SafeState based on the context provided (no CAN activity for configurable timeout).
Verify that the BMS_SystemState signal accurately reflects the HV Deactivating and subsequent states.

description:
IF  any cell temperature <= low temperature limit
THEN
  · BMS_CellUT_Flag := 1
  · BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 0 (charging prohibited)
supplementary_info:
Function: Thermal Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection
Context: Low temperature protection prevents charging when cells are too cold to accept current without damage, and manages the battery heater to enable charging under cold conditions. The heater is de-energized on overtemperature or when no longer needed:
- BMS_CellUT_Flag = 1: any cell <= low temperature limit (charging prohibited, BMS_ChargeReady_Flag = 0)
- BMS_HeaterOT_Flag = 1: heater temperature exceeds safe limit
- Heater activation threshold: cell <= -10 C during charge session

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_CellUT_Flag := 1 when any cell temperature <= low temperature limit; clearing: BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 0 (charging prohibited)
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where a single cell temperature is <= low temperature limit, causing BMS_CellUT_Flag to be set and BMS_ChargeReady_Flag to be cleared.
Test the boundary condition where exactly one cell's temperature equals the low temperature limit, ensuring that only BMS_CellUT_Flag is triggered while BMS_ChargeReady_Flag remains unaffected.
Test the fault condition where multiple cells are <= low temperature limit simultaneously to ensure both BMS_CellUT_Flag and BMS_ChargeReady_Flag are correctly set.
Test the transition from normal operation to under-temperature protection mode, including heater activation if necessary.
Verify that the system responds appropriately when a cell temperature is above the low limit threshold, ensuring BMS_CellUT_Flag and BMS_ChargeReady_Flag are cleared.

description:
The BMS shall continuously track:
  · Capacity fade (current vs. rated capacity)
  · DC internal resistance trend
  · Cell voltage deviation under load
supplementary_info:
Function: Degradation Monitoring
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation > 9.2 Charge Termination > 10 Pack Voltage Protection > 10.1 Pack Voltage Monitoring > 11 Degradation Monitoring > 11.1 Cell Degradation Detection
Context: Degradation monitoring tracks long-term cell aging through multiple indicators to detect capacity loss and impedance increase before they affect system performance. The SOH prediction model is updated when any indicator exceeds its threshold:
- Capacity fade: current capacity vs. rated capacity
- Impedance growth: DC internal resistance trend
- Voltage deviation: cell voltage dispersion under load
- BMS_CellDegrade_Warn = 1: any indicator exceeds defined threshold

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_CellDegrade_Warn = 1 when any indicator exceeds its threshold.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal operation where all indicators are within their thresholds, and BMS_CellDegrade_Warn remains 0.
Test the boundary condition where one of the indicators (capacity fade, impedance growth, or voltage deviation) just exceeds its threshold, causing BMS_CellDegrade_Warn to trigger from 0 to 1.
Test a scenario where multiple indicators exceed their thresholds simultaneously and verify that BMS_CellDegrade_Warn triggers correctly.
Verify the state transition of BMS_CellDegrade_Warn from 0 to 1 when an indicator exceeds its threshold, and back to 0 when it falls below the threshold again (if applicable).
Test that the system accurately monitors and records the indicators for capacity fade, impedance growth, and voltage deviation under load.

description:
Upon charger connection detection (CP/PP or CC signal):
  · Verify all safety preconditions
  · IF all preconditions satisfied
    THEN BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 1
  · ELSE BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 0, report reason on CAN
supplementary_info:
Function: Charge Management
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation
Context: Charge initiation is gated by a set of safety preconditions verified after charger connection. The BMS signals readiness on CAN once all preconditions are satisfied. If any precondition is not met, the reason is reported on CAN for diagnostic and HMI purposes:
- BMS_ChargeReady_Flag = 1: charger detected, no active faults, temperature in range, pack voltage in range
- Preconditions checked: active faults (sections 1-8), temperature limits (section 3), OVP state (section 1), undervoltage (section 8)

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 1 if all preconditions are satisfied; BMS_ChargeReady_Flag := 0 if any precondition is not met.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Verify that BMS_ChargeReady_Flag is set to 1 when all safety preconditions are satisfied upon charger connection.
Verify that BMS_ChargeReady_Flag remains at 0 if any of the safety preconditions are not met during charger connection detection.
Simulate an active fault condition and verify that BMS_ChargeReady_Flag is set to 0 with the appropriate fault reason reported on CAN.
Verify the state transition of BMS_ChargeReady_Flag from undefined to 1 or 0 based on charger connection detection and preconditions satisfaction.
Test that the correct reason for BMS_ChargeReady_Flag being set to 0 is reported on CAN when a safety precondition fails during charger connection detection.

description:
IF  total pack voltage < minimum operating threshold
THEN
  · BMS_PackUV_Flag := 1
  · Send DTC P0A01 to VCU
  · Limit discharge power proportionally to undervoltage severity
supplementary_info:
Function: Pack Voltage Protection
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring > 8 Undervoltage Protection > 8.1 Detection and Response > 9 Charge Management > 9.1 Charge Initiation > 9.2 Charge Termination > 10 Pack Voltage Protection > 10.1 Pack Voltage Monitoring
Context: Pack voltage monitoring provides a system-level check that complements individual cell monitoring (section 8). A pack undervoltage or sensor plausibility fault is reported to the VCU with DTC. The pack voltage measurement is cross-checked against the sum of cell voltages to detect sensor faults:
- BMS_PackUV_Flag = 1: pack voltage < minimum operating threshold, DTC P0A01 sent
- BMS_PackVoltSensorFault_Flag = 1: cell-sum to pack measurement deviation > 5 V

missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

Test a scenario where the cell-sum to pack measurement deviation is greater than 5 V but the total pack voltage is above the minimum operating threshold. Ensure that BMS_PackUV_Flag does not trigger in this case.
Test the transition from normal operation to the fault condition and back, ensuring proper flag setting and DTC reporting.
Verify that the system correctly reports BMS_PackUV_Flag status changes to the VCU.

description:
IF  VCU heartbeat signal lost > 500 ms
THEN
  · BMS_VCULost_Flag := 1
  · Revert to default safe-state assumptions
supplementary_info:
Function: Communication & Diagnostics
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection > 7 Communication and Diagnostics > 7.1 CAN Bus Fault > 7.2 VCU Heartbeat Monitoring
Context: VCU heartbeat monitoring ensures the BMS can detect loss of coordination with the vehicle controller and transition to a safe default state. A two-stage response provides a short grace period for transient communication loss before contactor-level action:
- BMS_VCULost_Flag = 1: heartbeat lost > 500 ms (revert to safe defaults)
- Main contactors open if BMS_VCULost_Flag persists >= 1 s
- BMS_SystemState := SafeState on contactor open

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_VCULost_Flag is set to 1 when VCU heartbeat signal is lost for more than 500 ms.
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where BMS_VCULost_Flag is set to 1 after a VCU heartbeat signal loss of more than 500 ms.
Test the boundary condition where BMS_VCULost_Flag is not set when the VCU heartbeat signal loss is exactly 500 ms.
Test the fault condition where BMS_VCULost_Flag remains unset after a VCU heartbeat signal loss of less than 500 ms.

description:
IF  HV leakage current > safety threshold
THEN
  · BMS_HVLeakage_Flag := 1
  · Trigger audible warning via HMI
supplementary_info:
Function: Insulation & HVIL
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring > 4.2 SOH Degradation Monitoring > 5 Cell Balancing > 5.1 Balancing Detection and Activation > 6 Insulation and HVIL > 6.1 Insulation Resistance Monitoring > 6.2 HV Interlock Monitoring > 6.3 HV Leakage Detection
Context: HV leakage monitoring provides an additional layer of protection beyond isolation resistance by detecting active current leakage through insulation breakdown. An internal fault in the monitoring device itself is also detected and reported. If a redundant measurement path is available, monitoring continues:
- BMS_HVLeakage_Flag = 1: leakage current > safety threshold, HMI warning triggered
- BMS_InsulationSensorFault_Flag = 1: monitoring device internal fault

analysis.direction: triggering
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior where HV leakage current exceeds the safety threshold and BMS_HVLeakage_Flag is set to 1, triggering an audible warning via HMI.

Test the scenario where the monitoring device itself fails and BMS_InsulationSensorFault_Flag is set to 1, indicating an internal fault in the leakage detection system.
Test the state transition from normal operation to a condition where both BMS_HVLeakage_Flag and BMS_InsulationSensorFault_Flag are triggered simultaneously due to severe insulation failure.

case_intents.description: Test the normal behavior when BMS_HVILFault_Flag is set to 1. Verify that all main contactors are opened within 100 ms and BMS_SystemState transitions to SafeState.
Test the boundary condition where BMS_HVILFault_Flag changes from 0 to 1 at exactly 100 ms. Ensure that the system responds correctly without any delay or premature actions.
Simulate a scenario where BMS_HVILFault_Flag is set to 1 but does not change back within 100 ms. Verify that the system still opens all main contactors and transitions to SafeState as expected.
Test the state transition from any other state to SafeState when BMS_HVILFault_Flag is set to 1. Ensure that the system handles this transition correctly without any issues.
Verify the observability of the BMS_HVILFault_Flag signal by checking its value before and after triggering the fault condition. Ensure that the flag accurately reflects the fault state.

description:
IF  calculated SOC <= 20 %
THEN
  · BMS_LowSOC_Warn := 1
  · Command instrument cluster low battery lamp ON
supplementary_info:
Function: SOC/SOH Estimation
Section: 1 Overvoltage Protection > 1.1 Detection and Confirmation > 1.2 Warning and Derating > 1.3 Severe Fault and Recovery > 2 Current Management > 2.1 Overcurrent Detection > 2.2 Short Circuit Detection > 2.3 Current Sensor Fault > 3 Thermal Management > 3.1 Over-Temperature Protection > 3.2 Under-Temperature Protection > 3.3 Temperature Imbalance > 4 SOC/SOH Estimation > 4.1 Low SOC Monitoring
Context: SOC monitoring provides the driver with range information and protects the battery from deep discharge by enforcing progressive power limits. The two-level warning scheme distinguishes between driver notification and actionable limitation:
- BMS_LowSOC_Warn = 1: SOC <= 20 % (advisory, instrument cluster lamp ON)
- BMS_LowSOC_Flag = 1: SOC <= 10 % (discharge limited to 25 % of MaxDischargePower)

analysis.direction: triggering: BMS_LowSOC_Warn := 1 when calculated SOC <= 20 %
missing_critical_info: response timing / debounce duration not specified

Test the normal behavior of not setting BMS_LowSOC_Warn to 1 when the calculated SOC is above 20%.
